titleStmt.title: Letter from Hugh de Fellenberg Montgomery to Sir Richard Dawson Bates, 23 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Hugh de Fellenberg Montgomery

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Hugh de Fellenberg Montgomery (1844-1924). Montgomery was a lawyer and politician. Originally a Liberal and a strong supporter of Gladstone, Montgomery was also a firm Unionist, but by 1916 he believed that Ulster Unionists had no choice but to accept Lloyd George's proposal for a six-county Northern Ireland. The letter addressed to Sir Richard Dawson Bates (1876-1949). Bates was a solicitor and later Northern Ireland Cabinet Minister. Bates was also secretary of the Ulster Unionist Council (1906–21) and joint secretary of the Unionist Associations of Ireland from 1907. in addition to this, he helped to organise the Ulster covenant and Ulster Volunteer Force. This letter concerns Montgomery's annual subscription to the Ulster Unionist Council of three guineas.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Copy 23rd June, 1916. Dear Mr. Bates, I enclose cheque for my 3 guineas, annual subscription
to the Ulster Unionist Council. Yours very truly,
